entry.title: IELTS Speaking кё°м¶ң
entry.content:
Part1вҖӢ

FishingвҖӢ

  • Is fishing popular in your country?
    • popular, a lot of lakes and rivers, enjoy fishing there
  • Do you like eating fish?
    • love sushi and sasimi, fish for dinner with
  • Have you ever been to a place where there are lots of fish around you?
    • Han River, a lot of small fish swimming in the river, вқҢ catch them, the water seemed dirty
  • Have you seen any movies with lots of fish?
    • Finding nemo, tropical fishes
  • Why do people go fishing?
    • enjoy the experience, eat the fish by cooking it

RunningвҖӢ

  • Do you like running?
    • like running, used to run around, a lot on my plate.
  • Where do you go running?
    • ліҙлқјл§Ө park, 5mins/on foot/ліҙлқјл§Ө station
  • How often do you go running?
    • Once a week, prefer walking to running, safey exercise for my back and knees
    • Once a week, used to run once a month, want to do exercise more
  • Which places do you think are perfect for running?
    • ліҙлқјл§Ө park, a running track, run with others
  • Do you think running is a good way to stay healthy?
    • feel a sence of achievement, relieve my stress

Coffee/TeaвҖӢ

  • Do you like to drink coffee or tea?
    • drink it every morning, prefer hand-dripped coffee to instant coffee
  • Do most people prefer tea or coffee today?
    • wake up by drinking coffee, enjoy the taste of coffee
  • How often do you drink coffee or tea?
    • drink coffee every single day, make hand-dripped coffee and take it
  • Are coffee and tea popular in your country?
    • the most popular beverage, on every corner, at least twice a day

Part2вҖӢ

activityвҖӢ

# Describe an activity you did with another person

- What the activity was: Riding a bike
- Where and where you did it: from ліҙлқјл§Ө to м—¬мқҳлҸ„, bike path
- Who you did it with: wife
- and why you chose that person
  - like her: a lot in common
    - exercise regularly
    - have the same taste in food
  - felt compfotable: know each other in and out
# Describe an activity that made you feel tired

- What the activity was: cleaning my home
- When and where it was: about this time, last month, home
- Why you took part in it:
  - took in charge of cleaning bathroom
  - better than my wife
- And explain why it made you feel tired
  - clean the floor: brush every tiles
  - get wet: although carefully, ended up getting wet

personвҖӢ

Describe a successful person you know

- Who they are: мҶҗнқҘлҜј, soccer player for Tottenham, a team that belongs to the English Premier League.
- What they do: stricker, one of the best players in his team.
- How you learnt about them: through media: TV and Internet news, these days advertisements.
- And explain why you think they are successful
  - famous: a World cup player, a member of the national team.
  - rich: according to the news, his salary is over 100 million dollars.

Part3вҖӢ

activitiyвҖӢ

  • Can you give some other examples of adventurous or risky activities?
    • transparent cable car.
    • scared but overcame the fear.
  • Why do some young people like adventurous activities?
    • like feeling thrills
    • make adventurous friends.
    • prefer to get together with those who are not cowards.
    • prove thier strength by doing adventurous activities.
  • Do older people like adventurous activities?
    • some older people like
    • to keep their health
    • but others are physically old.
    • they can't go out to do.
  • How do adventurous activities help people to relieve stress?
    • feel a sense of achievement.
    • challenging activities make them fully satisfied.
    • relieve stress.
  • What skills do people need to take part in adventurous activities?
    • In water activities: how to swim.
    • In outdoor activities: have strength.
  • Why do young people like doing exciting things?
    • relieve stress from schoolwork.
    • catch up with their friends.
  • How does friendship affect peopleвҖҷs overall well-being?
    • make more active.
    • more opp.
    • avoid loneliness.

Tiredness / EnergyвҖӢ

  • Why do people feel tired in todayвҖҷs world?
    • Using eletronic devices
    • Heavy workload
  • Does studying and learning make people tired today?
    • causes back pain
    • sit on the chair all day
    • can feel tired easily
  • When do people usually feel tired?
    • Heavy workload
    • In korea, people tend to work overtime
    • didn't get sleep well
    • suffer from sleep disorders
  • Do people have fewer holidays now than in the past?
    • in the past: should work 6 days a week
    • for now: work 5 days a week
    • people today have more holidays
  • What are the differences between feeling tired after studying and after exercising?
    • after studying: mentally tired
      • memorized many things
    • after exercising: physically tired
      • used their muscles
  • How can people solve the problem that old people easily get tired?
    • First: get some rest frequently
      • recover their body
    • Second: grow their strength
      • walking is the best way
  • Can technology, such as smartphones and computers, contribute to feeling tired? How?
    • Smartphones: people always watch them bending their necks
      • increase muscle tension
      • Even if they should sleep at night, they watch it overnight
      • causes tiredness and sleep disorders

FlawsвҖӢ

  • Some young people prefer to get together with those who are not cowards.
  • They want to prove their strength by engaging in adventurous activities.
  • I think it varies, depending on people.
  • but others are physically old, so they can't go out to do adventurous activities.
  • By doing adventurous activities, people can feel a sense of achievement.
  • These challenging activities make them fully satisfied.
  • In water activities, they should learn how to swim.
  • In the case of outdoor activities like climbing, they should have strength, particularly in their arms.
  • They can catch up with their friends by doing exciting things.
  • Friendship makes people more active.
  • They usually have more opportunities to meet others. This helps them avoid loneliness.
  • one of the best players in his league.
  • a member of the national team.
  • According to the news, his salary is over 100 million dollars.
  • They want to wake up by drinking coffee.
  • In the morning, I always make hand-dripped coffee and take it when I go to the academy or work.
  • There are coffee shops on every corner.

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • YourIELTS

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - лҚ°мқҙн„°лІ мқҙмҠӨ
entry.content:
DB к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

лҚ°мқҙн„°/м •ліҙ/м§ҖмӢқ/м§ҖнҳңвҖӢ

DB/DB systemвҖӢ

лҚ°мқҙн„° лҸ…лҰҪм„ұвҖӢ

  • л…јлҰ¬м Ғ лҸ…лҰҪм„ұ
  • л¬јлҰ¬м Ғ лҸ…лҰҪм„ұ

DBMSвҖӢ

DB м„Өкі„вҖӢ

DB мҡҙмҳҒвҖӢ

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • DA к°Җмқҙл“ң: н•ңкөӯлҚ°мқҙн„°мӮ°м—…м§„нқҘмӣҗ

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ
entry.content:
м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ м •мқҳвҖӢ

м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ FWвҖӢ

OWASP 10лҢҖ м·Ём•Ҫм җвҖӢ

м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ мң„нҳ‘вҖӢ

м •ліҙліҙнҳё нҶөм ң нҷңлҸҷвҖӢ

кҙҖлҰ¬м Ғ ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

CC мқёмҰқвҖӢ

  • Common Criteria мқёмҰқ

ISO 27001вҖӢ

ISMS-PвҖӢ

BCPвҖӢ

  • Business Continuity Planning
  • RTO: Recovery Time Objective
    • м„ңл№„мҠӨ мһ¬к°ңлҘј мң„н•ң лӘ©н‘ң ліөкө¬ мӢңк°„
    • кёҲмңөкё°кҙҖ: 2h
  • RPO: Recovery Point Objective
    • лӘ©н‘ң ліөкө¬ мӢңм җ лҚ°мқҙн„° мҶҗмӢӨмқ„ мӢңк°„мңјлЎң нҷҳмӮ°н•ң к°ңл…җ
    • кёҲмңөкё°кҙҖ: 0

DRPвҖӢ

  • Disaster Recovery Planning

л¬јлҰ¬м Ғ ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

мғқмІҙ мқёмӢқвҖӢ

  • Biometrics

кё°мҲ м Ғ ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

кё°мҲ м Ғ ліҙм•Ҳ м •мқҳвҖӢ

  • мқёмҰқ, м ‘к·јм ңм–ҙ, м•”нҳёнҷ”

м•”нҳёнҷ” мў…лҘҳвҖӢ

DESвҖӢ

  • Data Encryption Standard
  • Feistal кө¬мЎ°
  • н•ңкөӯ SEED 128bit -> ARIA 128bit SPN кө¬мЎ°

RSAвҖӢ

  • Rivest Shamir Adleman

нӮӨкөҗнҷҳ л°©мӢқвҖӢ

Diffie-HellmanвҖӢ

KDC л°©мӢқвҖӢ

  • Key Distribution Center

м „мһҗм„ңлӘ…вҖӢ

мқҙмӨ‘м„ңлӘ…вҖӢ

2FAвҖӢ

PKIвҖӢ

  • Public Key Infrastructure

SSOвҖӢ

  • Single Sign On
  • SSO --к¶Ңн•ңкҙҖлҰ¬--> EAM --кі„м •кҙҖлҰ¬--> IAM
    • Enterprise Access Mgmt.
    • Identity & Access Mgmt.

SAMLвҖӢ

  • Security Assertion Markup Language

OAuthвҖӢ

AAAвҖӢ

  • Authentication / Authorization / Accounting

OTPвҖӢ

  • One Time Password

м ‘к·јм ңм–ҙвҖӢ

  • лІЁлқјнҢҢл‘ҳлқј
  • Biba Integrity
  • нҒҙлқҪ & мңҢмҠЁ

FirewallвҖӢ

IDSвҖӢ

  • Instrusion Detection System: м№Ёмһ…нғҗм§ҖмӢңмҠӨн…ң

IPSвҖӢ

  • Instrusion Protection System: м№Ёмһ…л°©м§ҖмӢңмҠӨн…ң

IPSecвҖӢ

  • Internet Protocol Security Protocol

SSL/TLSвҖӢ

  • Secure socket Layer / Transport Layer Security

VPNвҖӢ

  • Virtual Private Network

NACвҖӢ

  • Network Access Control

UTMвҖӢ

  • Unified Threat Management

ESMвҖӢ

  • Enterprise Security Management

TMSвҖӢ

  • Threat Management System

мңөн•© ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

л¬ҙм„ л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

лӢЁл§җліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

DLPвҖӢ

  • Data Loss Prevention

MDMвҖӢ

  • Mobile Device Management

л””м§Җн„ё м»Ён…җмё  ліҙнҳёкё°мҲ вҖӢ

DOIвҖӢ

  • Digital Object Identifier

INDECSвҖӢ

  • Interoperability of Data in E-Commerce System: м „мһҗмғҒкұ°лһҳмқҳ м •ліҙ көҗнҷҳмқ„ мң„н•ң м§Җм Ғ мһ¬мӮ°к¶Ң кҙҖл Ё л©”нғҖлҚ°мқҙн„° лӘЁлҚё

л””м§Җн„ё мӣҢн„°л§ҲнҒ¬вҖӢ

н•‘кұ°н”„лҰ°нҠёвҖӢ

DRMвҖӢ

  • Digital Right Management

CASвҖӢ

  • Conditional Access System

Secure OSвҖӢ

HardeningвҖӢ

мҠӨл§ҲнҠёнҸ° ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

нҒҙлқјмҡ°л“ңм»ҙн“ЁнҢ… ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

м»ҙн“Ён„° нҸ¬л ҢмӢқвҖӢ

л””м§Җн„ёмҰқкұ°к°ңмӢңм ңвҖӢ

  • e-Discovery

мң л№„мҝјн„°мҠӨ м»ҙн“ЁнҢ… ліҙм•ҲвҖӢ

к°ңмқём •ліҙліҙнҳёвҖӢ

м •ліҙліҙм•Ҳ кҙҖл Ё лІ•вҖӢ

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - мҶҢн”„нҠёмӣЁм–ҙ кіөн•ҷ
entry.content:
SW кіөн•ҷ к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

SW к°ңл…җвҖӢ

SW мң„кё°вҖӢ

SW кіөн•ҷвҖӢ

SDLCвҖӢ

  • Software Development Life Cycle.

нҸӯнҸ¬мҲҳлӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

н”„лЎңнҶ нғҖмқҙн•‘вҖӢ

лӮҳм„ нҳ•лӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

л°ҳліөм җмҰқлӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

л°ҳліөм Ғ к°ңл°ң лӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

  • Iteration Model.

м җмҰқм Ғ к°ңл°ң лӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

  • Incremental Development Model.

м§„нҷ”лӘЁлҚёвҖӢ

  • Evolutionary Development Model.

нҒҙлҰ°лЈёкіөн•ҷвҖӢ

  • Clean Room Process Model.

к°ңл°ң л°©лІ•лЎ вҖӢ

кө¬мЎ°м Ғкё°лІ•вҖӢ

м •ліҙкіөн•ҷвҖӢ

к°қмІҙм§Җн–ҘвҖӢ

м»ҙнҸ¬л„ҢнҠёкё°л°ҳвҖӢ

  • CBD: Component Based Development

лӘЁлҚёкё°л°ҳ/м„ңл№„мҠӨкё°л°ҳвҖӢ

MDAвҖӢ

  • Model Driven Architenture.

SOAвҖӢ

  • Service Oriented Architecture.

MSAвҖӢ

  • Micro Service Architecture.

Agile л°©лІ•лЎ вҖӢ

  • ASD: Agile Software Development.

XPвҖӢ

  • eXtreme Programming

TDDвҖӢ

  • Test Driven Development

ScrumвҖӢ

LEANвҖӢ

DevOpsвҖӢ

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • м „мһҗм •л¶Җн”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬ көҗмңЎмӢ мІӯ

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - н”„лЎңм қнҠёкҙҖлҰ¬
entry.content:
н”„лЎңм қнҠё кҙҖлҰ¬ к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мӢӨнҢЁ нҳ„нҷ©вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мӢӨнҢЁ мӣҗмқёл¶„м„қвҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё м„ұкіө мҡ”мқёвҖӢ

PMBOKвҖӢ

  • Project Management Body Of Knowledge.
  • н”„лЎңм қнҠё кҙҖлҰ¬мқҳ мӮ¬мӢӨн•ӯ н‘ңмӨҖ.

н”„лЎңм қнҠё кҙҖлҰ¬ н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨвҖӢ

PMвҖӢ

  • Project Manager

м—ӯн• кіј мұ…мһ„вҖӢ

мқҙн•ҙкҙҖкі„мһҗл“ӨвҖӢ

мЎ°м§ҒмІҙкі„вҖӢ

кҙҖлҰ¬нҶөм ң мҠӨнӮ¬вҖӢ

мӮ¬нҡҢ, кІҪм ң, нҷҳкІҪвҖӢ

PMOвҖӢ

  • Project Management Office

м •мқҳмҷҖ н•„мҡ”м„ұвҖӢ

PMO ModelвҖӢ

PMO FrameworkвҖӢ

PMO мӮ¬лЎҖвҖӢ

м „мһҗм •л¶Җ PMOвҖӢ

лҸ„мһ…мӢңкё°лі„ мһҘлӢЁм җвҖӢ

м „мһҗм •л¶Җ PMO FrameworkвҖӢ

мҲҳн–үлӢЁкі„лі„ м„ёл¶Җ м—…л¬ҙвҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё лӢЁкі„лі„ нҷңлҸҷ мӮ¬лЎҖвҖӢ

н•өмӢ¬ м„ұкіө мҡ”мҶҢвҖӢ

KAвҖӢ

  • Knowledge Area

н”„лЎңм қнҠё нҶөн•©кҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё лІ”мң„кҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мқјм •кҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мӣҗк°ҖкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё н’Ҳм§ҲкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мһҗмӣҗкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мқҳмӮ¬мҶҢнҶөкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мң„н—ҳкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мЎ°лӢ¬кҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

н”„лЎңм қнҠё мқҙн•ҙкҙҖкі„мһҗкҙҖлҰ¬вҖӢ

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • Youtube: IT м „л¬ёк°Җ - н”„лЎңм қнҠёкҙҖлҰ¬ мӢңлҰ¬мҰҲ

entry.title: IELTS Listening
entry.content:
мң нҳ•вҖӢ

мҳҒм—ӯлӮҙмҡ©мң нҳ•л№Ҳм¶ң мЈјм ң
м„№м…ҳ1мқјмғҒлҢҖнҷ”к°•мқҳмӢ мІӯ, м—¬н–үл¬ёмқҳ
м„№м…ҳ2мқјмғҒлҸ…л°ұкҙҖкҙ‘м§Җ, н—¬мҠӨнҒҙлҹҪ, лҸ„м„ңкҙҖ
м„№м…ҳ3н•ҷмҲ лҢҖнҷ”мЎ°лі„кіјм ң, нҠңн„°л§Ғ
м„№м…ҳ4н•ҷмҲ лҸ…л°ұлҸҷмӢқл¬ј, л№„м§ҖлӢҲмҠӨ, м—ӯмӮ¬

кіөл¶ҖлІ•вҖӢ

  1. н•ңлІҲ л“Јкё°
  2. л‘җлІҲ л“Јкё°
  3. м •лӢө л§һм¶”кё°
  4. л”•н…Ңмқҙм…ҳ
  5. л”•н…Ңмқҙм…ҳ м •лӢөл§һм¶”кё°
  6. лӘ» л“ӨмқҖ кұ° мүҗлҸ„мһү

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - мңөн•© IT
entry.content:
мңөн•© IT к°ңмҡ”вҖӢ

л””м§Җн„ё м»ЁлІ„м „мҠӨвҖӢ

мңөн•©ліөмһЎлҸ„вҖӢ

мЈјмҡ” мҳҒм—ӯвҖӢ

к°ҖнҠёл„ҲвҖӢ

мӮ°м—… м Ғмҡ©вҖӢ

м„ңл№„мҠӨ мңөн•©вҖӢ

м„ңл№„мҠӨ м •мқҳвҖӢ

SOAвҖӢ

  • Service Oriented Architecture

ESBвҖӢ

  • Enterprise Service Bus

EDAвҖӢ

  • Event Driven Architecture

Web ServiceвҖӢ

SOA нҠёл Ңл“ңвҖӢ

Grid computingвҖӢ

Utility computingвҖӢ

SBCвҖӢ

  • Server Based Computing

Cloud computingвҖӢ

м»Ён…җмё  мңөн•©вҖӢ

м»Ён…җмё  м •мқҳвҖӢ

Web2.0вҖӢ

Semantic WebвҖӢ

LodвҖӢ

  • Linked Open Data

Open APIвҖӢ

Mash upвҖӢ

HTML5вҖӢ

л””л°”мқҙмҠӨ мңөн•©вҖӢ

N-screen serviceвҖӢ

DLNAвҖӢ

  • Digital Living Network Alliance

Mobile OfficeвҖӢ

GPGPUвҖӢ

  • General-Purpose computing on Graphics Processing Units

CUDAвҖӢ

Device APIвҖӢ

Hybrid AppвҖӢ

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • Gartner Top 10 Strategic Technology Trends for 2023
  • Youtube: IT м „л¬ёк°Җ - мңөн•©IT мӢңлҰ¬мҰҲ
  • IT кІҪмҳҒ

entry.title: IELTS Speaking
entry.content:
кіөл¶Җ л°©лІ•вҖӢ

  • л…№мқҢ
    • мІ« л¬ёмһҘ л§җн•ҳкё°
    • л‘җ лІҲм§ё л¬ёмһҘ л§җн•ҳкё°
    • м„ё лІҲм§ё л¬ёмһҘ л§җн•ҳкё°
  • л”•н…Ңмқҙм…ҳ
  • мҳӨлӢөл…ёнҠё, лёҢл ҲмқёмҠӨнҶ л°Қ
  • л°ҳліө

Part1вҖӢ

  • Well, м§Ғм ‘м Ғмқё лӢөліҖ
  • This is because
  • One reason is that
  • In the case of ~
  • To be specific,
  • What I mean is that
  • For example,
  • In fact,

Do you ever?вҖӢ

  • мҠөкҙҖ м§Ҳл¬ёмқҙлқј tend to, tend not to

Part2вҖӢ

  • нӮӨмӣҢл“ң, мӢңм ң нҢҢм•…
  • к°Җмқҙл“ң м§Ҳл¬ё нҷ•мқё
  • 1л¶„ л…ёнҠё н…ҢмқҙнӮ№

Part3вҖӢ

  • Well, I can think of 2 reasons / 2 ways
  • Well, I think there have been many changes

мһ¬м§Ҳл¬ёвҖӢ

  • лӘ» л“Өм—Ҳм–ҙ: Sorry, Could you please repeat the question?
  • л№Ёлһҗм–ҙ: Sorry, Could you please slow down a little?
  • лӢЁм–ҙлҘј лӘЁлҘҙкІ м–ҙ: Sorry, Could you please rephrase the question?

entry.title: IELTS Writing
entry.content:
Part1вҖӢ

FormatвҖӢ

Map нҢЁн„ҙвҖӢ

be p.p. be м—җлҠ” was, has been (now), will be (future)

  • ліҖкІҪ
    • A be replaced by B: мӮ¬л¬јмқҖ by мқҙлӢӨ.
    • A be converted into B
  • м¶”к°Җ
    • A be built
    • A constructred
    • A developed
  • нҢҢкҙҙ
    • A be demolished
  • мһ”мЎҙ
    • remained unchanged
  • мң„м№ҳ н‘ңнҳ„
    • next to кұҙл¬ј
    • behind кұҙл¬ј
    • on the north/west/south/east side of кұҙл¬ј,л§Ҳмқ„,м§ҖлӘ…
  • л§Ҳмқ„
    • the town, the park, the island

Process нҢЁн„ҙвҖӢ

  • мў…лҘҳ, нҳ•нғң, мҲҳмқјм№ҳ, мӢңм ң нҢҢм•….
  • In the # stage / progess / step / phase.
  • мҲҳлҸҷ: к°ҖкіөмӢқн’Ҳ, м ңн’Ҳ
    • is p.p. are p.p.
    • cheese, smoked fish, canned fruit, cemment...
  • лҠҘлҸҷ: мғқл¬ј, мһҗм—°нҳ„мғҒ: salmon, moth, water, CO2, rock
    • lay eggs -> hatch -> trow into -> migrate

Part2вҖӢ

мһ…мһҘ, к·јкұ°, мҳҲмӢң, м„ӨлӘ…, м •лҰ¬

TypesвҖӢ

  • м°¬м„ұ лҳҗлҠ” л°ҳлҢҖ: Agree / Disagree
  • л‘җ к°Җм§Җ кІ¬н•ҙ: Both Views
  • мһҘм җкіј лӢЁм җ: Advantage & Disadvantage
  • мӣҗмқё / л¬ём ңм җкіј н•ҙкІ°мұ…: Cause / Problem & Solution
  • л‘җ к°Җм§Җ кіјм ң: Two-part Question

FormatвҖӢ

GrammerвҖӢ

  • The number increased ... and the figure reached
    • === , with the figure reaching
    • лӢӨлҘё лӘ©м Ғм–ҙлҘј м—°кІ°н•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ°
  • The number increased ... and it reached
    • === , reaching
    • к°ҷмқҖ мЈјм–ҙлҘј м—°кІ°н•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ°

entry.title: кё°мҲ мӮ¬ - ITкІҪмҳҒ
entry.content:
ITкё°нҡҚ л°Ҹ мЎ°м§Ғнҷ”вҖӢ

Computer paradoxвҖӢ

CIOвҖӢ

  • Chief Information Officer

IT GovernanceвҖӢ

COBITвҖӢ

  • Control Objectives for Information and Related Technologies

ISO 38500вҖӢ

IT BSCвҖӢ

  • IT Balanced Score Card

IT portfolioвҖӢ

IT нҲ¬мһҗнҸүк°Җкё°лІ•вҖӢ

ISPвҖӢ

  • Information Strategy Planning

EAвҖӢ

  • Enterprise Architecture

ISMPвҖӢ

  • Information SYstem Master Plan

ITлҸ„мһ…вҖӢ

  • System Integration

ISO 12207вҖӢ

PMBOKвҖӢ

  • Project Mangement Body of knowledge

PRINCE2вҖӢ

  • Project In Controlled Environment

CMMIвҖӢ

  • Capability Maturity Model Integration

ITмҡҙмҳҒвҖӢ

  • System Management

ITSMвҖӢ

  • IT Service Management

ISO 20000вҖӢ

ITILвҖӢ

  • ITIL V2
  • ITIL V3
  • ITIL V4

м°ёмЎ°вҖӢ

  • Youtube: IT м „л¬ёк°Җ - ITкІҪмҳҒ мӢңлҰ¬мҰҲ
  • мңөн•© IT
